title: Rich Malaysian Salmon Wrap
description: A umami-packed, molten salmon wrap infused with hojicha tea and fresh ssam ingredients, perfect for a date‑night Malaysian feast.
instructions: Whisk together hojicha tea, soy sauce, lime juice, minced garlic, grated ginger, and sesame oil to make a glaze.
Place salmon fillet on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with half of the glaze.
Bake salmon for 12-15 minutes until just cooked, then remove and let rest.
Meanwhile, soak rice paper sheets in warm water for 10 seconds until pliable.
Lay a softened rice paper on a clean surface, then spread a thin layer of remaining glaze on the center.
Top with a slice of baked salmon, julienned cucumber, fresh mint, cilantro, and a pinch of chili flakes.
Fold the sides of the rice paper over the filling and roll tightly, sealing the edge with a dab of water.
Serve the wraps on a flat plate, drizzled with a small spoonful of extra glaze and a wedge of lime.
Enjoy the molten, umami-rich flavor of this Malaysian-inspired salmon wrap.
